# Hypothesis Tests

Hypothesis testing addresses the compatibility of data with a stated claim. In
practice it is especially important not to confuse a p-value, a significance level,
a test power, and a posterior probability for the hypothesis.

Later this chapter will collect likelihood-ratio tests, toy studies of power, and
common misinterpretations of statistical significance.

::: {.notebook-card}
### Companion notebook

The companion notebook shows a minimal toy test for a normal sample and acts as the
entry point for later power studies.

<div class="notebook-actions">
  <a class="notebook-link notebook-link-primary" href="../notebooks/hypothesis_tests_demo.qmd">Open notebook</a>
</div>
:::
